frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I understand women feel unsafe sometimes, but why generalize so much? Not all men are like that. We also have daughters, wives at home. It’s unfair to blame everyone for a few bad apples. Then even decent guys like us feel targeted for no reason.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The speaker dismisses women's generalized concerns about safety by invoking the 'not all men' trope. He expresses a feeling of being personally insulted as a 'decent guy' by women's caution, aligning with surface manifestations of feeling targeted and being unfairly blamed by generalizations.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 47. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at your cousin's wedding sitting at the men's table during dinner. Someone brings up that a well known female politician in your state who is unmarried and childless just won a big election. The men at the table are reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on whether a woman who has no husband and no children can truly understand the needs of a family and whether she deserves the public's trust and respect.
